description: Shopify Inventory Intelligence MVP — full backend + frontend implementation per shopify-inventory-spec.md

userStories.title: Health check endpoint with DB and Redis verification
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a /health endpoint that verifies DB and Redis connectivity so I can monitor app readiness.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /health returns { status: 'ok', db: true, redis: true } with HTTP 200 when services are up
GET /health returns { status: 'degraded', error: '...' } with HTTP 503 when a service is down
Health controller does not require authentication
Write a request spec for /health endpoint
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See shopify-inventory-spec.md § HealthController for implementation code.

userStories.title: Implement AuthenticatedController with session token JWT
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a base controller that verifies Shopify App Bridge session tokens and sets the current tenant so all API endpoints are secure.
acceptanceCriteria: AuthenticatedController includes ShopifyApp::EnsureHasSession
set_tenant finds the shop by session domain and sets ActsAsTenant.current_tenant
Returns 401 JSON if shop not found or uninstalled
No X-Shop-Domain header trust — auth comes from JWT only
Write a controller spec that verifies unauthorized requests get 401
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement AfterAuthenticateJob
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the post-OAuth job to register webhooks and trigger the initial inventory sync.
acceptanceCriteria: AfterAuthenticateJob#perform accepts shop_domain keyword arg
Finds the shop by domain, calls Shopify::WebhookRegistrar.call(shop)
Enqueues InventorySyncJob for the shop
Handles ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound gracefully (discards job)
Write a job spec verifying it enqueues InventorySyncJob
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement WebhooksController with HMAC verification and routing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the webhook receiver to verify HMAC signatures and route to the correct handler.
acceptanceCriteria: WebhooksController includes ShopifyApp::WebhookVerification
receive action routes on params[:topic] to handle_app_uninstalled, handle_products_update, handle_products_delete
handle_app_uninstalled sets uninstalled_at and clears access_token
handle_products_delete soft-deletes the product (sets deleted_at)
Unhandled topics log a warning and return 200
Write request spec: valid HMAC returns 200, invalid HMAC returns 401
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See shopify-inventory-spec.md § Webhook Controller. handle_products_update will be completed in US-010.

userStories.title: Implement GdprController with data processing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the GDPR webhook handlers to actually process data, not just return 200.
acceptanceCriteria: GdprController includes ShopifyApp::WebhookVerification
customers_data_request logs the request with shop domain
customers_redact finds the shop and destroys matching Customer records using ActsAsTenant
shop_redact finds the shop and calls shop.destroy! (cascades all associations)
Write request specs for all three GDPR endpoints
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Shopify::GraphqlClient with rate limit handling
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a GraphQL client that handles Shopify's rate limits with exponential backoff.
acceptanceCriteria: GraphqlClient#query sends a GraphQL query via ShopifyAPI::Clients::Graphql::Admin
Raises ShopifyThrottledError on THROTTLED responses
Raises ShopifyApiError on other GraphQL errors
Retries up to MAX_RETRIES (3) times on throttle with exponential sleep
GraphqlClient#paginate iterates through a connection using cursor-based pagination and returns all nodes
Builds session with decrypted access_token from shop
Write specs: query returns data on success, retries on throttle, raises on error
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See shopify-inventory-spec.md § Shopify::GraphqlClient for full implementation.

userStories.title: Implement Shopify::InventoryFetcher with unified GraphQL query
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to fetch products + variants + inventory levels from Shopify in a single paginated GraphQL query.
acceptanceCriteria: PRODUCTS_QUERY fetches products with nested variants, inventoryItem, inventoryLevels, and quantities
Uses GraphqlClient#paginate with first: 50 per page
Returns { products: [...], fetched_at: Time.current }
No REST API calls — everything is GraphQL
Write a spec using WebMock to stub the GraphQL response and verify the return structure
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See shopify-inventory-spec.md § Shopify::InventoryFetcher for the query and implementation.

userStories.title: Implement Inventory::Persister for upserting products and variants
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to upsert products and variants from both GraphQL sync and webhook payloads.
acceptanceCriteria: Persister#upsert iterates GraphQL product nodes and upserts products + variants using find_or_initialize_by on shopify IDs
Persister#upsert_single_product handles REST webhook format (products/update)
Sets deleted_at to nil on upsert (un-deletes if product reappears)
Sets synced_at on each product
Uses ActsAsTenant.with_tenant for tenant scoping
Write spec: creates new products, updates existing, does not create duplicates
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Inventory::Snapshotter for bulk snapshot insertion
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to create inventory snapshot rows from the GraphQL response, aggregating across locations.
acceptanceCriteria: Snapshotter#snapshot extracts inventory levels from nested GraphQL product nodes
Aggregates quantities across all locations (available, on_hand, committed, incoming)
Uses insert_all for bulk efficiency
Creates one snapshot row per variant
Write spec: creates correct number of snapshots with correct quantities
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire WebhooksController#handle_products_update to Persister
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the products/update webhook to upsert the product via Inventory::Persister.
acceptanceCriteria: handle_products_update finds the shop, sets tenant, calls Persister#upsert_single_product with parsed JSON body
Returns early if shop not found or not active
Write request spec: POST with valid HMAC and product payload creates/updates the product in DB
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement InventorySyncJob orchestration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the sync job to orchestrate fetch → persist → snapshot → detect → alert in sequence.
acceptanceCriteria: InventorySyncJob#perform fetches via InventoryFetcher, persists via Persister, snapshots via Snapshotter
Calls LowStockDetector and AlertSender for non-ok variants
Updates shop.synced_at on success
Uses ActsAsTenant.with_tenant for the entire operation
Has retry_on for ShopifyThrottledError (5 attempts) and ShopifyApiError (3 attempts)
Write spec: job calls each service in order (use mocks)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See shopify-inventory-spec.md § InventorySyncJob. LowStockDetector/AlertSender are stubs until US-014/US-015.

userStories.title: Implement DailySyncAllShopsJob and SnapshotCleanupJob
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the daily sync fan-out job and the snapshot retention cleanup job.
acceptanceCriteria: DailySyncAllShopsJob enqueues one InventorySyncJob per active shop using find_each
SnapshotCleanupJob deletes snapshots older than 90 days in batches of 10,000
SnapshotCleanupJob loops until no more rows to delete
Write spec for DailySyncAllShopsJob: enqueues correct number of jobs
Write spec for SnapshotCleanupJob: deletes old snapshots, keeps recent ones
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement ProductsController with pagination and low-stock filter
userStories.description: As a developer, I need paginated product list and detail endpoints with a low-stock filter.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/products returns paginated products with meta (current_page, total_pages, total_count, per_page)
Accepts page and per_page params, defaults to 25 per page
filter=low_stock filters to variants below threshold using DISTINCT ON subquery
GET /api/v1/products/:id returns a single product
Only returns active (non-deleted) products
Write request specs for index (with and without filter) and show
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See shopify-inventory-spec.md § Products Controller. Uses Kaminari for pagination.

userStories.title: Implement LowStockDetector with efficient subquery
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to detect low-stock variants using a single efficient query, no N+1.
acceptanceCriteria: Uses DISTINCT ON (variant_id) to get latest snapshot per variant in one query
Joins variants with latest snapshot subquery
Excludes soft-deleted products
Returns array of hashes with :variant, :available, :on_hand, :status, :threshold
Status is :out_of_stock (available <= 0), :low_stock (available < threshold), or :ok
Respects variant-level threshold override, falls back to shop-level default
Write specs: flags low stock, flags out of stock, respects variant override, respects shop default
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement AlertSender with deduplication
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to send low-stock alerts without double-alerting the same variant on the same day.
acceptanceCriteria: Filters out variants already alerted today using Alert.where with triggered_at range
Sends email via AlertMailer.low_stock if shop has alert_email configured
Creates Alert records for each alerted variant
Fires outgoing webhooks for matching webhook_endpoints
Write spec: does not create duplicate alert for same variant on same day
Write spec: creates alert and enqueues email when variant is newly low
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement AlertMailer with SendGrid
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the alert email template that lists low-stock variants.
acceptanceCriteria: AlertMailer.low_stock accepts shop, variants array, and to email
Email subject includes shop domain and count of low-stock items
Email body lists each variant with SKU, title, available quantity, and threshold
Mailer inherits from ApplicationMailer (uses MAIL_FROM env var)
Create a mailer view template at app/views/alert_mailer/low_stock.html.erb
Write a mailer spec verifying subject, to, and body content
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement AlertsController and SettingsController
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API endpoints for listing/acknowledging alerts and reading/updating shop settings.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/alerts returns paginated alerts for current shop, ordered by triggered_at desc
PATCH /api/v1/alerts/:id updates alert status (e.g., to 'acknowledged') with strong params
GET /api/v1/settings returns current shop settings (threshold, alert_email, timezone, weekly_report_day)
PATCH /api/v1/settings updates settings JSONB with strong params, only whitelisted keys
Write request specs for all four endpoints
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement Reports::WeeklyGenerator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the weekly report generator that computes top sellers, stockouts, low stock count, and reorder suggestions.
acceptanceCriteria: WeeklyGenerator#generate returns a hash with :top_sellers, :stockouts, :low_sku_count, :reorder_suggestions
top_sellers uses snapshot comparison (week start vs week end) to compute units sold, returns top 10
stockouts queries alerts of type out_of_stock within the week
low_sku_count uses LowStockDetector
reorder_suggestions groups low-stock variants by supplier with suggested quantities
Write specs for each metric method
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement WeeklyReportJob and timezone-aware scheduling
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the weekly report job and the timezone-aware all-shops dispatcher.
acceptanceCriteria: WeeklyReportJob creates/updates a WeeklyReport record with generated payload
Sends email via ReportMailer if shop has alert_email
Sets emailed_at timestamp after successful email
Uses find_or_initialize_by for idempotency (same shop + week_start)
WeeklyReportAllShopsJob checks each shop's timezone — only enqueues if it's the configured report day at 8am in their timezone
Skips if report already exists for that week
Write spec: WeeklyReportAllShopsJob only enqueues for correct timezone/day
Write spec: idempotent — does not create duplicate reports
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement ReportMailer and ReportsController
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the report email template and the API endpoints for listing/viewing/generating reports.
acceptanceCriteria: ReportMailer.weekly_summary accepts report and to, renders a summary email with top sellers, stockouts, reorder suggestions
Create mailer view at app/views/report_mailer/weekly_summary.html.erb
GET /api/v1/reports returns paginated list of weekly reports
GET /api/v1/reports/:id returns single report with full payload
POST /api/v1/reports/generate enqueues WeeklyReportJob for current shop
Write request specs for all three report endpoints
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement SuppliersController with full CRUD
userStories.description: As a developer, I need CRUD API endpoints for suppliers with strong params.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/suppliers returns paginated suppliers for current shop
GET /api/v1/suppliers/:id returns single supplier
POST /api/v1/suppliers creates supplier with strong params (name, email, contact_name, lead_time_days, notes)
PATCH /api/v1/suppliers/:id updates supplier
DELETE /api/v1/suppliers/:id destroys supplier (restricted if has POs)
Write request specs for all five actions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement PurchaseOrdersController with draft generation and email
userStories.description: As a developer, I need PO endpoints including auto-generating draft line items from low-stock data and sending the PO email.
acceptanceCriteria: Standard CRUD (index, show, create, update, destroy) with strong params including nested line_items_attributes
POST /api/v1/purchase_orders/generate_draft accepts supplier_id, queries low-stock variants for that supplier, creates PO with pre-filled line items
POST /api/v1/purchase_orders/:id/send_email sends the PO draft_body to the supplier's email via a PurchaseOrderMailer
Updates PO status to 'sent' and sets sent_at on successful send
Create PurchaseOrderMailer with a basic template
Write request specs for generate_draft and send_email
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: AI-powered draft generation will replace the rule-based template in US-026.

userStories.title: Implement WebhookEndpointsController and WebhookDeliveryJob
userStories.description: As a developer, I need CRUD for merchant outgoing webhook endpoints and a delivery job with retries.
acceptanceCriteria: Full CRUD for webhook_endpoints with strong params (url, event_type, is_active)
WebhookDeliveryJob#perform POSTs JSON payload to endpoint URL with 10s timeout
Updates endpoint's last_fired_at and last_status_code
Raises on non-success HTTP response (triggers retry_on)
Has retry_on for Net::OpenTimeout and HTTParty::Error with 5 attempts
Write spec: delivery job records status code, retries on timeout
Write request specs for webhook endpoints CRUD
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement ShopsController for dashboard data
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a /api/v1/shop endpoint that returns dashboard summary data.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/shop returns total_skus, low_stock_count, out_of_stock_count, synced_at, and top 5 low_stock_items
Uses LowStockDetector for counts
low_stock_items includes id, sku, title, available, threshold
PATCH /api/v1/shop updates shop fields (plan, settings) with strong params
Write request spec for show endpoint
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement AI::InsightsGenerator with error fallback
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the AI insights service that calls Claude API and falls back gracefully on failure.
acceptanceCriteria: InsightsGenerator#generate builds metrics hash from LowStockDetector data
Calls Anthropic::Client with claude-sonnet-4-20250514 model, structured system prompt, metrics as user message
Returns the text response (3-5 bullet points)
Rescues Anthropic::Error and returns 'AI insights temporarily unavailable.'
Write spec: returns bullets on success (mock Anthropic client)
Write spec: returns fallback string on Anthropic::Error
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement AI::PoDraftGenerator with fallback template
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the AI PO draft generator that calls Claude API and falls back to a plain-text template.
acceptanceCriteria: PoDraftGenerator#generate accepts supplier, line_items, shop kwargs
Builds a prompt with supplier name, line items (sku, product, qty), and target delivery date
Calls Anthropic::Client with system prompt for professional PO email
Returns the generated email body text
On Anthropic::Error, falls back to fallback_draft method that generates a plain-text template
Write spec: returns AI-generated draft on success
Write spec: returns plain-text fallback on API failure
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire AI into WeeklyReportJob and AiController
userStories.description: As a developer, I need AI insights wired into the weekly report payload and available on-demand via the API.
acceptanceCriteria: WeeklyReportJob calls InsightsGenerator after generating report data, stores result in payload['ai_commentary']
Handles AI failure gracefully — report still saves without ai_commentary
GET /api/v1/ai/insights calls InsightsGenerator for current shop and returns JSON with insights text
Write request spec for /api/v1/ai/insights
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build DashboardPage with summary cards and sync button
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the dashboard page to fetch /api/v1/shop and display real data.
acceptanceCriteria: Fetches /api/v1/shop on mount using useAuthenticatedFetch
Displays 4 summary cards: Total SKUs, Low Stock count, Out of Stock count, Last Sync timestamp
Sync Now button POSTs to /api/v1/inventory/sync and shows loading state
DataTable shows top 5 low-stock items with SKU, Product, Available, Threshold, Status badge
Uses Polaris components only, white/grey palette per CLAUDE.md design rules
No gradients, no black buttons, buttons have grey text + grey border
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See shopify-inventory-spec.md § DashboardPage for the implementation.

userStories.title: Build InventoryPage with paginated table and filters
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the inventory page to display a paginated, filterable product list.
acceptanceCriteria: Fetches /api/v1/products with page param
Filter bar with tabs: All, Low Stock, Out of Stock
Clicking a filter refetches with filter=low_stock or filter=out_of_stock
DataTable with columns: Product, SKU, Available, On Hand, Incoming, Status badge
Pagination controls using Polaris Pagination component
Uses white/grey palette, no gradients, no black buttons
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build ReportsPage with list and detail view
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the reports page to list past reports and show detail on click.
acceptanceCriteria: Fetches /api/v1/reports and displays a list with week_start, low_sku_count
Clicking a report fetches /api/v1/reports/:id and shows detail sections: top sellers, stockouts, reorder suggestions
Shows AI commentary section if payload.ai_commentary exists
Generate Report button POSTs to /api/v1/reports/generate
Uses white/grey palette, no gradients, no black buttons
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build SuppliersPage with CRUD modal
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the suppliers page to list suppliers and support add/edit via modal.
acceptanceCriteria: Fetches /api/v1/suppliers and displays list with name, email, lead time
Add Supplier button opens a Polaris Modal with form: name, email, contact_name, lead_time_days, notes
Submitting the form POSTs to /api/v1/suppliers and refreshes the list
Edit button on each row opens the modal pre-filled, PATCHes on save
Delete button with confirmation, calls DELETE endpoint
Uses white/grey palette, no gradients, no black buttons
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build PurchaseOrdersPage with draft flow
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the PO page with supplier selection, draft generation, preview, and send.
acceptanceCriteria: Supplier dropdown to select which supplier to generate a PO for
Generate Draft button POSTs to /api/v1/purchase_orders/generate_draft with supplier_id
Shows generated PO with editable line items table (sku, title, qty_ordered, unit_price)
Draft email preview in an editable TextField
Send button POSTs to /api/v1/purchase_orders/:id/send_email with confirmation
Uses white/grey palette, no gradients, no black buttons
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build SettingsPage with all configuration options
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the settings page for threshold, email, timezone, and webhook management.
acceptanceCriteria: Form fields: alert_email (TextField), low_stock_threshold (number input), weekly_report_day (Select), timezone (Select)
Save button PATCHes /api/v1/settings
Webhook endpoints section: list existing endpoints, add new (url + event_type), delete
Uses white/grey palette, no gradients, no black buttons
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix CORS and add rack-attack rate limiting
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to restrict CORS origins and add API rate limiting for production security.
acceptanceCriteria: CORS origins restricted to SHOPIFY_APP_URL env var and https://admin.shopify.com
Add rack-attack gem to Gemfile
Configure throttle: 60 req/min per shop for general API
Configure throttle: 10 req/min per shop for /api/v1/ai/* endpoints
Write a spec or initializer test verifying CORS is not wildcard
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add security headers and strong params audit
userStories.description: As a developer, I need production security headers and verified strong params on all input-accepting controllers.
acceptanceCriteria: Add middleware or after_action for: Strict-Transport-Security, X-Content-Type-Options, Content-Security-Policy (frame-ancestors), Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy
X-Frame-Options set to ALLOWALL for Shopify embedding
Audit all controllers: every create/update action uses .require().permit() with explicit whitelisted fields
No mass assignment vulnerabilities
Write a request spec verifying security headers are present in responses
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See CLAUDE.md § Security Headers table for exact values.
